oai_dc:dc.dc:title: A Year of DIY Graduate Librarianship
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Jesse, Drake
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Drawing on experience at a university aspiring to R1 status, this article details a “DIY Graduate Librarianship” approach to supporting graduate students amidst institutional resource limitations. Faced with an understaffed library unable to fully support the university’s R1 mission, a librarian proactively developed services and supports. Initiatives included support for a cohort of PhD students, graduate writing assistance, and application support for prospective graduate students. This narrative highlights how individual initiative and strategic “yeses” can bridge service gaps, raise the library’s profile, and foster professional development, even when formal resources are constrained, paving the way for future collaborative graduate support.
oai_dc:dc.dc:publisher: Association for Information Science and Technology
